title: uprmdir
text:
uprmdir Return the direction (in/out/inout) of the specified parameter. Explain this code int uprmdir (UHACThOpr, intseqNr, int*pDir); Copy Parameters hOpr Handle to the operation, as returned by uinstopr seqNr Sequence number of the parameter (sequence numbers start with 1) pDir Direction of the parameter (Output) Return Values Table 1. Values returned by uprmdir Value Meaning 0 Success. -165 The supplied handle is not valid. -1203 The sequence number is out of range. The value returned in the variable pointed to by pDir can be one of the following: Table 2. Values returned (variable pDir) Value Mnemonic Parameter type 1 UPARM_INPUT IN 2 UPARM_OUTPUT OUT 3 UPARM_INPUT | UPARM_OUTPUT INOUT These mnemonics are defined in h3gl.h , and we recommend that you use them rather than the actual values. Use Library: ucall Description uprmdir returns the direction of the specified operation parameter. It does not matter whether there is parameter data attached to the operation parameter list. Example: uoprprms, uniname, uprmdir, and uprmtype The following routine prints out a list of the parameters of an arbitrary operation in an arbitrary component. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> char *types[] = { "Unknown", "String", "Boolean", "Numeric", "Float", "Date", "Time", "DateTime","LinearDate", "LinearTime", "LinearDateTime", "Raw", "Image", "Entity", "Occurrence", "IncludedEntity", "Handle" }; char *dires[] = { "Unknown", "In", "Out", "InOut" }; void inspect_operation_params (char *svcname, char *oprname) { UHACT hEnv, hIn, hOp, hParm; long i, nParams; int pType, pDir; char name[1+UPRMNAMESIZE]; char* template1 = " %3s %-20s %-20s %s
"; char* template2 = " %3d %-20s %-20s %s
"; ueopen(&hEnv); uinstnew(hEnv, (UCHAR*)svcname, 0, 0, UDEFAULT_COMM_MODE, 0, &hIn, 0); uinstopr(hIn, (UCHAR*)oprname, 0, &hOp); printf("Parameters of operation %s in service %s:

", oprname, svcname); printf(template1, "Num", "Name ", "Type ", "Direction"); printf(template1, "===", "====================", "====================", "========="); uoprprms(hOp, &nParams); for ( i=1; i<=nParams; i++ ) { uprmdir(hOp, i, &pDir); uprmgeth(hOp, i, 0, &hParm); uprmtype(hParm, &pType); uniname(hOp, i, UPRMNAMESIZE, name); printf(template2, i, name, types[pType], dires[pDir]); } printf("
"); uinstdel(hIn, 0); ufreeh(&hOp); ufreeh(&hIn); ufreeh(&hEnv); } Copy Here is an example of output generated by this function: Explain this code Parameters of operation MYOP in service TESTSVC: Num Name Type Direction === ==================== ==================== ========= 1 P1 Numeric In 2 P2 String InOut 3 P3 Date Out Copy Related Links uoprprms Return the number of parameters in the specified operation. uprmgeth Get the handle of the specified operation parameter or entity field. Or detach a parameter from an operation. uprmtype Return the data type of the specified parameter or entity field. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
